Dhanendra Sahu is a senior Congress leader in Chhattisgarh and a five-time MLA from Abhanpur. He previously served as the president of the Chhattisgarh Pradesh Congress Committee from 2008 to 2011. In the 2024 election, he contested as an MLA candidate from Abhanpur but lost.
Rohit Sahu is an Indian politician from Chhattisgarh who won the 2023 Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly election as an MLA from the Rajim constituency, representing the Bharatiya Janata Party.

Janak Ram Dhruw is an Indian politician from Chhattisgarh, serving as MLA from the Bindranawagarh Assembly constituency, which is reserved for Scheduled Tribes. He has expressed concerns about Maoist threats in the region and sought enhanced security.
Ambika Markam is an Indian politician from the Indian National Congress. She was elected as an MLA from the Sihawa constituency in the 2023 Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly election.
Ajay Chandrakar is a five-time MLA and a former Cabinet Minister in the Government of Chhattisgarh.
Onkar Sahu won the 2023 Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly election as an MLA from the Dhamtari constituency, representing the Indian National Congress.
Sangeeta Sinha is an MLA from Sanjari Balod Assembly Constituency in Chhattisgarh, having won the 2023 Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly election representing the Indian National Congress.
Anila Bhendia is a two-time MLA from Dondi Lohara, Chhattisgarh, representing the Indian National Congress. She has served as the Minister of Child and Woman Welfare in the Chhattisgarh government.

Tamradhwaj Sahu is an Indian politician from the Indian National Congress (INC). He has served as a Member of Parliament (MP) and a Member of the Legislative Assembly (MLA) in Chhattisgarh and Madhya Pradesh. He was the Home Minister of Chhattisgarh from 2018 to 2023.
Bhupesh Baghel has been a prominent politician in Chhattisgarh, serving as Chief Minister from 2018 to 2023 and representing the Patan constituency in the Legislative Assembly multiple times.
Lalit Chandrakar is an MLA from the Durg Gramin Assembly constituency in Chhattisgarh, representing the Bharatiya Janata Party.

Renuka Singh Saruta is a three-time elected Member of the Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly representing Bharatpur-Sonhat as a Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) member. She previously served as the Minister of State for Tribal Affairs of India from 2019 to 2023.

Shyam Bihari Jaiswal is a BJP politician representing Manendragarh in the Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly since 2023. He serves as the Minister of Health and Family Welfare and Minister of Medical Education in the Chhattisgarh government.
Bhaiyalal Rajwade is a BJP MLA from the Baikunthpur constituency in Chhattisgarh, having won the 2023 election.
Bhulan Singh Marabi is an MLA from the Premnagar Assembly constituency in Surajpur district, Chhattisgarh, representing the Bharatiya Janata Party.
Laxmi Rajwade is the current Minister of Women and Child Development in Chhattisgarh, elected as MLA from Bhatgaon in the 2023 Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly election.
Shakuntala Singh Portey won the 2023 Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly election as an MLA from the Pratappur constituency, representing the Bharatiya Janata Party.
Rajesh Agrawal won the 2023 Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly election as an MLA from the Ambikapur constituency, representing the Bharatiya Janata Party.
Prabodh Minz won the 2023 Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly election as an MLA from the Lundra Assembly constituency, representing the Bharatiya Janata Party.
Raymuni Bhagat was elected as an MLA from the Jashpur Assembly constituency in Chhattisgarh in the 2023 Legislative Assembly election, representing the Bharatiya Janata Party.

Vishnu Deo Sai currently represents the Kunkuri constituency in the Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly, having won the seat in the 2023 state elections as a member of the Bharatiya Janata Party. He subsequently became the Chief Minister of Chhattisgarh in 2023.

Gomati Sai is an Indian politician from Chhattisgarh who served as an MP from Raigarh and was elected as an MLA from the Pathalgaon Assembly constituency, which is reserved for the Scheduled Tribe community in Jashpur District.
Sunita Singh Rathia won the 2013 Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly election from the Lailunga constituency as a candidate of the Indian National Congress.

Om Prakash Choudhary is an Indian politician and former IAS officer serving as the Minister of Finance in Chhattisgarh. He won the 2023 Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly election from the Raigarh constituency as a BJP candidate.

Uttari Ganpat Jangde is an Indian politician from Chhattisgarh who won the Sarangarh Assembly constituency in the 2018 Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly election representing the Indian National Congress.

Umesh Nandkumar Patel is an Indian politician from Chhattisgarh, representing the Kharsia constituency in the Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly as a member of the Indian National Congress. He has been the president of the Chhattisgarh Youth Congress Committee since 2016.
Laljeet Singh Rathia is a three-time MLA from the Dharamjaigarh Assembly constituency, reserved for the Scheduled Tribes community, in Raigarh district, Chhattisgarh.

Lakhan Lal Dewangan is a politician from Chhattisgarh, serving as Minister of Commerce and Industry and Labour in the state government. He represents the Korba constituency as an MLA and is a prominent figure in the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP).

Premchand Patel is a politician from the Bharatiya Janata Party representing the Katghora constituency in the Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly. He won the 2023 election by defeating Congress candidate Purushottam Kanwar by a margin of 16,900 votes.
Tuleshwar Hira Singh Markam won the 2023 Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly election from the Pali-Tanakhar constituency, representing the Gondwana Gantantra Party.
Pranav Kumar Marpachi is an Indian politician from Chhattisgarh who won the 2023 Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly election as a BJP candidate from the Marwahi (ST) constituency.
Atal Shrivastava is an Indian politician from Chhattisgarh, serving as MLA from the Kota Assembly constituency since winning the 2023 election under the Indian National Congress.

Arun Sao is the current 2nd Deputy Chief Minister of Chhattisgarh and represents the Lormi Vidhan Sabha seat in the 6th Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly since 2023. He previously served as a Member of Parliament (Lok Sabha) from Bilaspur, Chhattisgarh, from 2019 to 2023 and was the President of the BJP's Chhattisgarh unit from 2022 to 2023.

Punnulal Mohle is a long-serving politician from Chhattisgarh, currently representing Mungeli in the Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly as a member of the Bharatiya Janata Party. He has previously served as a Minister and as a legislator in Madhya Pradesh and the Lok Sabha.

Dharmjeet Singh Thakur is an Indian politician and current MLA from Takhatpur constituency in Chhattisgarh. He previously won the 2018 election from Lormi as a member of the Janta Congress Chhattisgarh.

Amar Agrawal is a member of the Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly representing Bilaspur constituency as a Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) candidate. He is a former Cabinet Minister in the Government of Chhattisgarh.

Dharamlal Kaushik is a BJP politician from Chhattisgarh, serving as MLA from Bilha since 2008 (except for the 2003 loss to Siyaram Kaushik). He was Speaker of the Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly (2009-2014) and Leader of Opposition (2019).
Dilip Lahariya is a two-time MLA from the Masturi Assembly constituency in Chhattisgarh. He won the 2023 Chhattisgarh Legislative Assembly election, representing the Indian National Congress.

Tanpho Wangnaw was elected as an MLA from the Longding–Pumao constituency in Arunachal Pradesh in the 2019 Legislative Assembly election, representing the Bharatiya Janata Party.

political_background.summary: Pradyumansinh Mahipatsinh Jadeja is a Gujarat MLA from Abdasa. He first won the seat in 2017 as an Indian National Congress candidate, later resigned from Congress in 2020 and joined the Bharatiya Janata Party. He won the 2020 by-election and was re-elected in the 2022 Gujarat Legislative Assembly election as a BJP candidate.

Chaitanya Makarandbhai Desai is an Indian politician from Gujarat and a member of the Bharatiya Janata Party. He was elected as a Member of the Gujarat Legislative Assembly from the Akota constituency in the 2022 Gujarat Assembly elections.

Dr. Hasmukh Patel is an Indian politician and medical professional from Gujarat. He is a member of the Bharatiya Janata Party and has been elected as the MLA from the Amraiwadi constituency in the Gujarat Legislative Assembly in 2017 and again in 2022.

Kaushik Kantibhai Vekariya is an Indian politician from Gujarat and a member of the Bharatiya Janata Party. He was elected as a Member of the Gujarat Legislative Assembly from the Amreli constituency in the 2022 Gujarat Assembly election.

Yogesh R. Patel is a senior Bharatiya Janata Party leader from Gujarat and a long-serving MLA from the Anand constituency. He has been elected multiple times since 1998 and has served as a minister in the Government of Gujarat, holding portfolios including Narmada Development, Water Resources, and Water Supply.

Chhanga Trikam Bijal is an Indian politician from Gujarat and a member of the Bharatiya Janata Party. He has been elected as a Member of the Gujarat Legislative Assembly from the Anjar constituency, winning the seat in the 2017 and 2022 Gujarat Assembly elections.

Amit Chavda is an Indian politician from Gujarat and a senior leader of the Indian National Congress. He has been elected multiple times as the MLA from the Anklav constituency since 2012. He previously served as the President of the Gujarat Pradesh Congress Committee and has been an active figure in state politics.

Ishwarsinh Thakorbhai Patel is an Indian politician from Gujarat and a member of the Bharatiya Janata Party. He has been elected as the Member of the Gujarat Legislative Assembly from the Ankleshwar constituency, winning the seat in the 2017 and 2022 Gujarat Assembly elections.
